Description.Name: Buried Riches
Description.Details:
<color=#BFBFBF>A short, text-only transmission arrives on the command line. No formal greeting or signature, but unmistakably from Arelle.</color>

<color=#DFDFDF>"Analysis underway. Initial scans of the convoy wreckage show shipments of an unidentified high-density metal alloy, extremely durable under heat and kinetic stress. Its origin appears natural, but its application is currently unknown. Further study required."</color>

<color=#BFBFBF>Another line appears moments later.</color>

<color=#DFDFDF>"The mine must be neutralized. It runs deep, too deep to collapse conventionally. However, its above-ground infrastructure includes all atmospheric regulation systems. Remove those, and operations will become untenable. Prepare for immediate deployment."</color>

<color=#BFBFBF>The transmission ends there. No follow-up, no context. Just orders and a mineral report that raises more questions than it answers.</color>

<color=#BFBFBF>After the bridge clears, Farah pulls you aside near the engineering console. She's holding a small sealed sample case.</color>

"Commander, I need to show you something. During the convoy engagement I managed to collect a few fragments from the wreckage before the [[DM.Factions[faction_GhostBearWatch],Watch]] recovery teams swept everything. Small pieces, nothing they would have noticed missing."

<color=#BFBFBF>She opens the case. Inside, three irregular shards of dark metal sit in padded slots. Even under the bay lighting they seem to absorb more light than they reflect.</color>

"I've run preliminary analysis. The alloy doesn't match any known construction or armor standard in our databases. The grain structure is unusual. Density is well above anything used in conventional fabrication, and the stress tolerances I'm measuring suggest a structural application. Something designed to bear enormous sustained load over long periods."

<color=#BFBFBF>She taps her console, pulling up a comparison chart.</color> "Whatever this material is intended for, it's being engineered for a scale far beyond standard construction. The tolerances don't make sense for buildings, vehicles, or anything in common use. It's overbuilt for every known application I can think of."

<color=#BFBFBF>She closes the sample case and looks at you.</color> "I don't know what it's for, Commander. But someone is mining this material in bulk and shipping it under heavy guard. That's not something you do for a research project. That's production."

Description.Name: Move on to the next contract
Move on to the next contract
Description.Details: [A new contract is available in the Command Center]
